tradeIdeas.title: Long Brent Crude / Long ASEAN Energy Producers
tradeIdeas.rationale: Hormuz effectively closed post-Feb 28 US-Israeli strikes. Brent spiked to $109.80 morning (Mar 10) on Kuwait halt, VLCC seizure, UN veto — then crashed -17.7% to $90.35 on Trump de-escalation signal ('war may end'). Entered at $79; base target revised to $100 (prior $115 and $120 targets HIT intraday); stress target $115. Military posture unchanged — Kuwait halt and Hormuz closure intact; Trump signals historically volatile. Consider trimming position on bounce but hold core long. Do not chase the intraday low.

tradeIdeas.title: Long ASEAN Banks / Short ASEAN Energy Equities
tradeIdeas.rationale: Trump de-escalation signal (Mar 10 PM): ASEAN Banks 203.5 (+3.9%); iTraxx IG tightened to 152bp (-16bp); ASEAN HY 480bp (-28bp). ASEAN Energy Equities 138.5 (-8.6%) — energy short gains crystallising as Brent crashes to $90.35. Trim energy short on de-escalation overshoot; extend bank long. Wilmar and IJM plantation longs partially offset by oil retreat but biodiesel mandate tailwind intact.

tradeIdeas.title: PHP Short — Elevated Conviction on Iran Shock
tradeIdeas.rationale: USD/PHP at 59.17 (-0.6% Mar 10 close) — Trump de-escalation signal rallied PHP below the 59.50 NDF strike; position now out-of-the-money. Philippines DOE emergency fuel rationing remains in effect; BSP monitoring Brent trajectory. At Brent $90.35, current account pressure eases materially vs $109.80. Consider rolling strike to 58.50 or closing if Brent sustains sub-$95. Re-enter on any re-escalation signal. MUFG stress target: 61.00; stop-loss at 57.50.

tradeIdeas.title: IDR Short — Dual Exposure to Iran Shock and Shadow Fleet Risk
tradeIdeas.rationale: IDR at 16,745 (-2.2% Mar 10 close) — sharp rally on Trump de-escalation signal reversed the morning's weakness. ID 10Y at 7.58% (-14bp). Bank Indonesia may resume rate cut consideration if Brent sustains sub-$95. Position moved against on de-escalation; consider reducing. Re-enter short on any re-escalation confirmation — structural oil import burden and OFAC enforcement tightening remain. Revised stress target 17,000–17,500; tail 18,000.

tradeIdeas.title: iTraxx Asia ex-Japan IG CDS Protection — Hold Position
tradeIdeas.rationale: iTraxx Asia ex-Japan IG at 152bp (-16bp Mar 10 close) — spreads tightened sharply on Trump de-escalation signal. CDS entered at 102bp — still +50bp in-the-money. ASEAN HY at 480bp (-28bp). Hold existing 5Y position — Kuwait halt and Hormuz closure structurally intact; Trump signals historically volatile. P&I blackout remains in effect; 329 tankers still trapped. 160–200bp credible stress target if re-escalation; 130bp stop-loss if full ceasefire confirmed. Sapura Energy and Hyflux remain most vulnerable ASEAN HY names.
instruments: Sapura Energy / Hyflux bond short via CDS

tradeIdeas.title: Long JKM LNG vs Short TTF — Spread Re-widening on Hormuz Shock
tradeIdeas.rationale: JKM at $15.71/MMBtu (-3.0% Mar 10 close) — de-escalation signal pulled JKM off the $16.20 morning high. TTF at €51.80/MWh (-4.4%). Qatar Ras Laffan: single cargo only; full restart contingent on conflict end. Hold JKM long — still +49% above 30d baseline ($10.52); structural LNG supply deficit unresolved. In 1–3 month extended closure, JKM could approach $22–25/MMBtu. VLCC Pacific Gallant seizure precedent keeps Malacca risk elevated regardless of Trump signal.
instruments: Asia LNG spot cargo purchase via intermediary

cfTriggers.description: CRITICAL: Sapura Energy MYR debt maturities 2026–2027 face acute refinancing risk. iTraxx Asia IG at 152bp (-16bp Mar 10) and ASEAN HY at 480bp (-28bp) — marginal improvement on Trump signal but bank refinancing window remains closed for HY credits. Brent at $90.35 still well above E&P capex thresholds; revenue uplift lags capex by 2–3 quarters. Shift immediately to private credit, DFI, or multilateral lenders. If Trump signal leads to ceasefire within 2–4 weeks and Brent retraces toward $70, reassess capital markets window.

THB bond maturity H2 2026 — TH 10Y at 3.38% (-13bp Mar 10) on Trump de-escalation. THB strengthened to 31.18 (-3.2%). Execute pre-financing now — rate window has improved and Brent at $90.35 still supports PTTEP upstream EBITDA materially above breakeven. If re-escalation resumes, TH 10Y could reprice 3.60%+. MAS FATF guidance requires Gulf of Oman JV compliance review. Kuwait halt and VLCC Pacific Gallant seizure remain in force pending any ceasefire.

Expand USD revolving credit facility this week. Bunker fuel ~12% of cost base — at Brent $90.35, annualised fuel cost delta vs Q4 2025 est. USD 580–640M (down from $700–780M at $109.80). iTraxx Asia IG at 152bp and improving — window has reopened slightly on Trump signal; execute RCF expansion before any re-escalation reversal. Biodiesel mandate tailwind (Indonesia B40, Malaysia B20) intact. Pre-emptive RCF expansion of USD 750–1,000M still recommended — do not wait for outcome clarity.

Base scenario upgraded to 28% on Trump de-escalation signal. Brent at $90.35 — energy cost covenant breach less acute than at $109.80 but still $20.35/bbl above the $70/bbl ICP assumption. ASEAN HY at 480bp (-28bp) — capital markets remain closed for unsecured HY debt. Engage restructuring advisor immediately — model two pathways: (a) sustained sub-$95 Brent under Trump off-ramp → assess capital markets window by Q3 2026; (b) re-escalation → private credit or DFI bridge only viable path. Do not wait for ceasefire confirmation before engaging advisors.
